title: What happens to the lanterns at the end of the festival?

meanings.usage: 指文化或宗教庆祝活动
通常指纸糊的照明灯具
the + 形容词，表示一类人
指从一个地方到另一个地方的路程
指引人注目的场景或表演
指聚集在一起的一群人
指海洋、湖泊或大河的岸边
指令人愉快的或受欢迎的
指运气不好或带来坏运气的
指令人感动或情绪激动的
指将物品整齐地摆放出来
指经过思考后形成某种看法
指认为某事会发生或应该发生

examples.en: He is said to be very rich.
They are expected to arrive tomorrow.
Food was laid out on the table.
The lanterns help the dead to find their way.
People dance and sing all night long.
In the early morning, the food is thrown into the river.
examples.zh: 一大早，食物被扔进河里。

examples.en: It is considered unlucky to eat the food.
examples.zh: 吃那些食物被认为是不吉利的。

examples.en: Thousands of lanterns drift out to sea.
examples.zh: 成千上万的灯笼漂向大海。

examples.en: The lanterns slowly drift out to sea.

phrases.phrase: until they can be seen no more

examples.en: They watch until they can be seen no more.
examples.zh: 他们看着直到看不见为止。

grammar.title: 一般过去时的被动语态
grammar.definition: 表示过去发生的动作，且主语是动作的承受者
grammar.structure: 主语 + was/were + 过去分词 + (by + 动作执行者)
grammar.usage: 当强调动作的承受者或不知道动作执行者时使用

examples.en: A Festival for the Dead is held once a year.
The food was thrown into a river.
The tiny lanterns were placed into the water.

grammar.title: be + 动词不定式（表示计划或安排）
grammar.definition: 表示按计划或安排将要发生的事
grammar.structure: 主语 + am/is/are + to + 动词原形
grammar.usage: 表示将来确定会发生的事或被安排的事

examples.en: The dead are said to return to their homes.
examples.zh: 据说死者会回到他们的家。

examples.en: The lanterns are to be placed into the water.

grammar.title: 时间状语从句
grammar.definition: 用来说明动作发生时间的从句
grammar.usage: 用于描述两个动作的时间先后关系

examples.en: People dance and sing when the festival is held.
examples.zh: 节日举行时人们唱歌跳舞。

examples.en: The lanterns are placed into the water when the festival is over.
examples.zh: 节日结束时，灯笼被放入水中。

sentencePatterns.pattern: It is considered + adj. + (for someone) + to do something

original.en: It is considered unlucky for anyone living to eat it.
original.zh: 对于任何活着的人来说，吃它被认为是不吉利的。

imitations.en: It is considered impolite to ask about someone's age.
imitations.zh: 询问别人的年龄被认为是不礼貌的。

imitations.en: It is considered dangerous to swim alone.
imitations.zh: 独自游泳被认为是有危险的。

original.en: Food is laid out for them.

imitations.en: Books are placed on the desk.

sentencePatterns.pattern: Thousands of + 复数名词 + 动词

original.en: Thousands of lanterns slowly drift out to sea.
original.zh: 成千上万的灯笼缓缓漂向大海。

imitations.en: Thousands of people gather at the square.
imitations.zh: 成千上万的人聚集在广场上。

imitations.en: Thousands of birds fly south in winter.
imitations.zh: 成千上万的鸟在冬天飞向南方。

sentencePatterns.pattern: ...until + 主语 + can + be + 过去分词 + no more

original.en: ...until they can be seen no more.

imitations.en: They watched the boat until it could be seen no more.
imitations.zh: 他们看着那艘船直到看不见为止。

imitations.en: She waited until the light could be seen no more.
imitations.zh: 她等待着直到看不见那光。
